assets.prompt: Hand-authored repo asset for the April 20 Amber Wall defender plant. Chunky amber/honey-tinted lane wall with stacked resin blocks so the new defender role reads as a tank, visually distinct from attackers (thorn motifs) and support (flower motifs).

Hand-authored repo asset for the April 21 Cottonburr Mortar plant. A rooted green mortar cup holding a pale cottonburr payload so the new arc-and-rearmost plant reads as a back-rank siege piece instead of a straight-line shooter.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 21 Cottonburr Mortar projectile. A spiked cottonburr seed with a warm core so arc shots read as a lobbed impact pod instead of a thorn bolt.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 19 Pollen Puff splash plant. Warm yellow puffball bloom with a soft pollen halo so the second anti-air plant reads as radius rather than a piercing line.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 19 Pollen Puff projectile. Glowing amber pollen orb with a dusty trail so splash bolts are visually distinct from Bramble Spear lances and Thorn Vine thorns.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 13 Bramble Spear roster plant. Distinct spear-petal silhouette with amber core so the second plant reads clearly even when asset generation times out.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 13 Bramble Spear projectile. Long bramble lance silhouette so piercing shots are visually distinct from Thorn Vine bolts.

Create a enemy sprite of briar-backed beetle predator with glowing amber core. hostile creature sprite for a top-down arena game, readable attack silhouette, visually distinct body shape, premium indie-game finish. modern high-detail pixel art, nuanced shading, crisp edges, visually rich but gameplay-readable, designed for a contemporary indie game. Style mode: topdown_asset. Output should hold up at 384x384 source resolution and still read clearly when scaled down in gameplay. Transparent or removable background. avoid chunky 8-bit nostalgia, avoid intentionally primitive low-detail treatment, avoid exaggerated CRT or old-console aesthetics.

Create an animated enemy sprite of briar beetle lane-pusher with amber shell plates and thorn legs. hostile lane-pushing creature for a top-down lane-defense game, readable attack silhouette, visually distinct body shape, premium indie-game finish. Render a cohesive low-framerate gameplay animation with consistent character volume, palette, and positioning across frames. Use the rd-animation style walking_and_idle. Each frame must keep the same subject identity, palette, and camera angle while showing one coherent gameplay action. Target frame size 48x48. No text, labels, borders, UI panels, or background scene dressing. modern polished pixel art, crisp readable silhouettes, consistent shading, and strong gameplay clarity. avoid muddy low-contrast frames, avoid random pose drift, avoid noisy background detail, avoid deliberately primitive 8-bit nostalgia.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 16 Briar Sniper ranged enemy. Crouched briar-mantis archer with a tensioned thorn crossbow aimed leftward so sniper threat reads as ranged.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 16 Briar Sniper projectile. Crimson thorn bolt with fletching so sniper shots are visually distinct from plant-owned projectiles.

Create an animated enemy sprite of crouched briar-mantis archer with a tensioned thorn crossbow aimed leftward. Hand-authored repo asset for the April 17 Frost Fern control plant. Cool-blue crystalline fern that emits a lane-zone chill; no animation.
Hand-authored frost flake particle for the Frost Fern chill trail emitter; cool-blue six-point snowflake silhouette.

User-authored Thornwing Moth spritesheet imported from /Users/dtannen/Desktop/moth.png and resized with nearest-neighbor scaling to a 4x4, 64x64-frame Phaser sheet for the April 18 flying enemy.

Hand-authored Loamspike Burrower walk+burrow spritesheet for the April 24 runtime. 4x4 grid of 64x64 frames; gameplay-facing walk row indices [12,13,14,15] match the existing walker enemy pattern (briar-beetle, shard-mite). Portrait reference lives at loamspike-walk.svg; the PNG is produced by the asset pipeline. Boot procedural fallback covers the texture until the PNG ships.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 24 Loamspike dive telegraph. Soil-crack decal anchored at the dive column so the player can read where the burrower is about to go under.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 24 Loamspike surface marker. Subtle ground-crack at the surface column, visible from telegraph through underpass so surface prediction is readable.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 24 Loamspike underpass shadow. Semi-transparent tracking shadow that follows the burrower's x-position while underground so the player can see where it is.

Hand-authored Husk Walker body for the April 26 front-armored ground enemy. Static SVG loaded as a single image (animation loop is overkill per brief — runtime motion plus the husk-walker-plate decal's scaleY 0.85 and y -4 offset convey the 600 ms vulnerability windup). When the matching PNG spritesheet (4x4, 64x64 frames; gameplay row indices [12,13,14,15] mirroring loamspike/glass-ram) ships from the asset pipeline, swap path/format and add metadata.phaser.frameWidth/frameHeight=64; until then applyEnemyAnimationFrame's setFrame call no-ops harmlessly on the single-image texture.

Hand-authored repo asset for the April 26 Husk Walker plate decal. Single-frame plate-only overlay aligned over the husk-walker body; runtime sets scaleY=0.85 and y -=4 during the vulnerability windup so the plate visibly retracts and the soft chest is exposed.
Hand-authored repo asset for the April 24 Loamspike surface dust burst. One-shot puffball decal emitted when the burrower emerges from underpass.
